sections.paragraph: i.q. Ch. מְהַלּ, Heb. מוּל (comp. p. 211, A ), to cut off, to prune; figuratively, to adulterate, to spoil, to mix wine with water, Isa 1:22. In like manner the Arabs apply verbs of cutting, breaking, wounding, killing; and Martial, Ep. i. 18, jugulare vetat Falernum. See more in my Comment. on Isa. loc. cit.
